seoTitle: How to Make Uneven Columns in Google Docs
metaDescription: Learn how to create asymmetrical, unequal-width columns in Google Docs for newsletters, brochures, or side-by-side comparisons.
intro: Google Docs defaults to equal-width columns, but you can override that to build a newsletter, brochure, or comparison layout with uneven column widths. This guide walks through the formatting menu options needed to set precise custom widths. It's for anyone designing a document that needs asymmetrical text layout.

steps.heading: Select your text and open column options
steps.body: Highlight the paragraph text you want to format, click Format, hover over Columns, and click More Options.

steps.heading: Unlock variable widths
steps.body: Set the number of columns to two and clear the Equal Column Widths checkbox to unlock independent sizing.

steps.heading: Enter custom widths
steps.body: Type precise inch values into the column width fields to create your asymmetrical split.

steps.heading: Add a dividing line
steps.body: Check the Line Between Columns box if you want a clean vertical border separating the sections.

steps.heading: Apply and fine-tune
steps.body: Click Apply to commit the formatting, then use Insert, Break, Column Break to control where text jumps between columns, adjusting the ruler sliders as needed.

faq.question: Can I apply uneven columns to just part of a page?
faq.answer: Yes, using a Section Break lets you isolate a multi-column layout to a specific portion of your document rather than the whole page.

faq.question: How do I force text into the next column early?
faq.answer: Place your cursor where you want the break, then insert a Column Break to push remaining text into the next column.

faq.question: Can I adjust column widths with my mouse instead of typing values?
faq.answer: Yes, the interactive ruler bar at the top lets you drag margin sliders to adjust column widths visually.

faq.question: Does this work the same on mobile?
faq.answer: Advanced column formatting options are generally easier to access through the desktop web version of Google Docs.
